Title: Create a new role with no inherited roles
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator with Manage Roles privilege; on Administration > Manage Roles
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Role | 2. Enter role name | 3. Enter description | 4. Add a single privilege | 5. Click Save
Test_Data: Role=Front Desk Clerk; Description=Handles patient check-in; Privilege=Add Patients
Expected_Result: Role is created and appears in the Manage Roles list with the assigned privilege and description

Title: Create a role that inherits from a parent role
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; role Nurse exists with clinical privileges
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Role | 2. Enter role name | 3. Select Nurse as inherited role | 4. Click Save | 5. Open the new role and view effective privileges
Expected_Result: New role is created and inherits all privileges from Nurse plus any directly assigned ones

Title: Reject creating a role with a duplicate name
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; role Nurse already exists
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Role | 2. Enter the name of an existing role | 3. Add a privilege | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the save with a validation error indicating the role name already exists; no duplicate role is created

Title: Reject creating a role with an empty role name
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; on Add Role screen
Test_Steps: 1. Leave role name blank | 2. Enter a description | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: System blocks the save and displays a required-field validation error for role name

Title: Role name field boundary at maximum allowed length
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; on Add Role screen
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a role name at the maximum allowed character length | 2. Add a privilege | 3. Click Save | 4. Re-open the role to verify persistence
Expected_Result: Role is saved without truncation and persists at exactly the maximum length on reload

Title: Prevent deletion of a role currently assigned to active users
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; role Clerk is assigned to at least one active user
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Roles | 2. Select role Clerk | 3. Click Delete | 4. Confirm the prompt
Expected_Result: System prevents deletion or warns that the role is in use; assigned users are not orphaned and retain a valid access state

Title: Delete an unused custom role
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; custom role Temp Role exists and is assigned to no users
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Roles | 2. Select Temp Role | 3. Click Delete | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Role is removed from the Manage Roles list and can no longer be assigned

Title: Create a new privilege and assign it to a role
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; on Administration > Manage Privileges
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Privilege | 2. Enter privilege name | 3. Enter description | 4. Save | 5. Open a role and assign the new privilege | 6. Save the role
Expected_Result: Privilege is created and successfully assignable to a role; the role reflects the new privilege

Title: Reject creating a privilege with a duplicate name
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; privilege Add Patients exists
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Privilege | 2. Enter Add Patients | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the duplicate privilege name with a validation error

Title: Create a new user and assign a single role
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator with Manage Users privilege; on Administration > Manage Users > Add User
Test_Steps: 1. Search/create the associated person | 2. Enter username | 3. Set a strong password | 4. Confirm password | 5. Assign role Nurse | 6. Click Save
Expected_Result: User account is created, appears in Manage Users, and can log in with the Nurse role privileges

Title: Create a user with multiple roles assigned
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; roles Nurse and Data Manager exist
Test_Steps: 1. Add User | 2. Enter username | 3. Set password and confirm | 4. Assign Nurse and Data Manager | 5. Save | 6. Log in as the new user and verify combined access
Expected_Result: User receives the union of privileges from both roles and can access features from each

Title: Reject creating a user with a duplicate username
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; user jnurse already exists
Test_Steps: 1. Add User | 2. Enter username jnurse | 3. Set password and confirm | 4. Assign a role | 5. Click Save
Expected_Result: System blocks creation and shows a validation error that the username is already taken

Title: Reject weak password not meeting complexity policy
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; password policy requires length, upper, lower, and digit
Test_Steps: 1. Add User | 2. Enter username | 3. Enter a weak password | 4. Confirm | 5. Click Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the password with a complexity policy error and does not create the user

Title: Reject mismatched password and confirmation
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; on Add User screen
Test_Steps: 1. Enter username | 2. Enter password | 3. Enter a different confirmation password | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: System blocks save and displays a password mismatch validation error

Title: Create user with no role assigned (least privilege default)
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; on Add User screen
Test_Steps: 1. Enter username | 2. Set password and confirm | 3. Assign no roles | 4. Save | 5. Log in as that user
Expected_Result: User can authenticate but has only baseline Anonymous/Authenticated privileges and cannot access privileged apps

Title: Disable (retire) an active user account
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; active user jnurse exists and can log in
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Users | 2. Select jnurse | 3. Click Disable/Retire | 4. Enter a retire reason | 5. Save | 6. Attempt to log in as jnurse
Expected_Result: Account transitions to disabled; subsequent login attempts are rejected with an account-disabled message

Title: Re-enable a previously disabled user account
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; user jnurse is currently disabled/retired
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Users | 2. Select jnurse | 3. Click Enable/Unretire | 4. Save | 5. Log in as jnurse
Expected_Result: Account transitions back to active and the user can log in with previously assigned roles intact

Title: Active session of a user invalidated after account disable
Preconditions: User jnurse is logged in with an active session in browser B; administrator in browser A
Test_Steps: 1. In browser A disable jnurse | 2. In browser B perform a privileged action or navigate | 3. Observe session handling
Expected_Result: The disabled user is denied further privileged actions and is forced to re-authenticate or is logged out

Title: Reset a user password as administrator
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; user jnurse exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Users | 2. Select jnurse | 3. Click Change Password | 4. Enter a new strong password | 5. Confirm | 6. Save | 7. Log in as jnurse with new password
Expected_Result: Password is updated; user can log in with the new password and the old password no longer works

Title: Registration Clerk sees only registration apps on home dashboard
Preconditions: Logged in as a user with only the Registration Clerk role
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the clerk | 2. Observe the home dashboard app tiles | 3. Enumerate visible apps
Expected_Result: Only registration-related apps (e.g. Register a Patient, Find Patient) are visible; clinical and admin apps are hidden

Title: Doctor sees clinical apps but not system administration
Preconditions: Logged in as a user with only the Clinician/Doctor role
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the doctor | 2. Inspect home dashboard tiles | 3. Attempt to locate the System Administration app
Expected_Result: Clinical apps are visible; the System Administration tile is not displayed for the doctor

Title: System Administrator sees the full administration menu
Preconditions: Logged in as a user with the System Developer/Administrator role
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Open the System Administration app | 3. Enumerate available admin sections
Expected_Result: All administration sections (Manage Users, Roles, Privileges, Global Properties) are visible and accessible

Title: Clerk without prescribe privilege cannot access ordering
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk lacking the Add Orders privilege; a patient chart is open
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient dashboard | 2. Attempt to open the Orders/Drug Order entry | 3. Observe access
Expected_Result: Order entry is not available to the clerk; the action is blocked with insufficient privileges

Title: Doctor with prescribe privilege can place a drug order
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with Add Orders privilege; patient chart open with an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard | 2. Start a visit if needed | 3. Open Drug Orders | 4. Add a medication | 5. Sign/save the order
Expected_Result: Drug order is accepted and saved, appearing in the patient active orders list

Title: Nurse cannot void/delete an encounter without Delete Encounters privilege
Preconditions: Logged in as Nurse without Delete Encounters privilege; an encounter exists on a patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard | 2. Open an existing encounter | 3. Attempt to void/delete it
Expected_Result: The void/delete control is hidden or the action is denied with an insufficient-privileges error

Title: Clerk cannot edit clinical observations
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; patient has recorded vitals/observations
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard | 2. Locate vitals/observations | 3. Attempt to edit an observation value
Expected_Result: Editing controls for clinical observations are not available to the clerk; the action is blocked

Title: User with View Patients but not Edit Patients can view but not modify demographics
Preconditions: Logged in as a user holding View Patients but not Edit Patients privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient demographics | 2. Confirm details are readable | 3. Attempt to open the edit demographics form
Expected_Result: Demographics are visible read-only; the edit action is unavailable or denied

Title: Privilege matrix across roles for core patient actions
Preconditions: Test users provisioned for Clerk, Nurse, Doctor, Pharmacist, Admin; one shared test patient
Test_Steps: 1. For each role attempt Register, Edit Demographics, Record Vitals, Prescribe, Void Encounter | 2. Record allowed/denied per cell | 3. Compare against the expected matrix
Expected_Result: Each role allows only the actions in its expected matrix row; all other actions are denied consistently

Title: Direct URL navigation to admin page by non-admin is denied
Preconditions: Logged in as Nurse (non-admin); admin user-management URL is known
Test_Steps: 1. While logged in as Nurse, paste the admin Manage Users URL into the address bar | 2. Submit | 3. Observe the response
Expected_Result: Access is denied (redirect to an error/login or insufficient-privilege page); the admin page is not rendered

Title: Forced browsing to delete action endpoint is blocked server-side
Preconditions: Logged in as Nurse without Delete privileges; a delete endpoint pattern is known
Test_Steps: 1. Capture a delete request URL/parameters | 2. Replay it as the nurse session | 3. Observe server response and whether data changed
Expected_Result: Server rejects the request with a 403/insufficient-privilege response and no data is deleted

Title: Non-admin user cannot grant themselves additional roles
Preconditions: Logged in as a standard Nurse user without Manage Users privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to open own user account edit screen | 2. Attempt to add the System Administrator role to self via UI or API | 3. Observe outcome
Expected_Result: Self-escalation is blocked; the user cannot modify their own role assignments and no new role is granted

Title: Vertical escalation via tampered role parameter on save user is rejected
Preconditions: Logged in as a Data Manager who can edit some users but not assign admin role; intercept proxy available
Test_Steps: 1. Open edit user for a low-privilege account | 2. Intercept the save request | 3. Inject the System Administrator role id | 4. Forward the request
Expected_Result: Server validates authorization and rejects the elevated role assignment; the target user does not receive admin

Title: Horizontal access prevention across provider scope
Preconditions: Two clinicians at different locations; patient belongs to provider A only
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as provider B | 2. Attempt to open provider A restricted patient or order via direct id | 3. Observe access
Test_Data: Provider=B; Patient owned by Provider A=Test-006
Expected_Result: Provider B is prevented from accessing or modifying the other provider's restricted record per location/scope rules

Title: Admin action of creating a user is recorded in the audit log
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; audit/logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Create a new user | 2. Open the audit/admin action log or query the audit table | 3. Locate the create-user entry
Expected_Result: An audit entry records the actor, action type, target user, and timestamp for the user creation

Title: Role assignment change is captured with before/after values
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; audit logging enabled; user jnurse exists
Test_Steps: 1. Add a role to jnurse and save | 2. Inspect the audit log entry | 3. Verify before and after role sets are captured
Expected_Result: Audit log shows the role change with actor, timestamp, and the prior and new role assignments

Title: Failed unauthorized access attempts are logged for review
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; a non-admin user available
Test_Steps: 1. As a non-admin attempt to access an admin endpoint and get denied | 2. Query the security/audit log | 3. Locate the denied-access record
Expected_Result: A log entry records the denied access attempt with user, target resource, and timestamp

Title: Account locks after configured consecutive failed logins
Preconditions: Lockout policy configured for N failed attempts; user jnurse exists
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt login with wrong password N-1 times | 2. Verify account still active | 3. Submit one more wrong attempt | 4. Then submit the correct password
Expected_Result: Account is unlocked at N-1 failures and becomes locked exactly at the Nth failure; correct password is rejected while locked

Title: Successful login resets the failed-attempt counter
Preconditions: Lockout policy configured; user jnurse has some recent failed attempts below threshold
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a few wrong passwords below threshold | 2. Submit the correct password to log in | 3. Log out | 4. Submit wrong passwords again and count to lock
Expected_Result: Failed-attempt counter resets after a successful login; lockout requires a fresh full count of failures

Title: Idle session timeout enforces re-authentication
Preconditions: Session timeout configured; user logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as any user | 2. Leave the session idle beyond the timeout | 3. Attempt a privileged action
Expected_Result: Session expires and the user is required to re-authenticate before any privileged action proceeds

Title: Pairwise combinations of role, location scope, and account status
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; multiple locations configured
Test_Steps: 1. Provision users covering pairwise combos of {Clerk,Nurse,Doctor} x {Loc-A,Loc-B} x {Active,Disabled} | 2. Attempt a representative action per combo | 3. Record results
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination behaves per policy: disabled users denied entirely; active users limited to role-appropriate actions at their location

Title: REST API rejects privileged call without authentication
Preconditions: OpenMRS REST module enabled; no session/credentials provided
Test_Steps: 1. Send a GET to a protected REST resource without auth header | 2. Observe HTTP status and body
Expected_Result: API responds 401 Unauthorized and returns no protected user data

Title: REST API denies action when authenticated user lacks privilege
Preconditions: OpenMRS REST module enabled; credentials for a Nurse without Manage Users
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as the nurse | 2. POST to create a user via REST | 3. Observe status and result
Expected_Result: API responds 403 Forbidden and no user is created

Title: REST API allows privileged call for authorized admin
Preconditions: OpenMRS REST module enabled; admin credentials available
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as admin via Basic Auth | 2. GET the list of users | 3. Validate response payload
Expected_Result: API responds 200 and returns the user list the admin is authorized to see

Title: Expired or invalid session token is rejected
Preconditions: A previously valid session token that has since expired or been invalidated
Test_Steps: 1. Issue a REST call using the stale token | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: API rejects the request with 401 and does not return protected data

Title: FHIR Patient read denied without SMART scope
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint protected by SMART on FHIR; token lacks patient/Patient.read scope
Test_Steps: 1. Request GET /fhir/Patient/{id} with a token missing the read scope | 2. Observe status and OperationOutcome
Expected_Result: Server returns 403 with an OperationOutcome indicating insufficient scope; no patient data is returned

Title: FHIR write scope required to create a resource
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint with SMART scopes; token has read but not write scope
Test_Steps: 1. POST a new Observation with a read-only token | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: Server returns 403 and does not persist the Observation; OperationOutcome cites missing write scope

Title: Patient-scoped token cannot access another patient's record
Preconditions: SMART patient-context token bound to Patient/123
Test_Steps: 1. Use the patient-context token to GET Patient/999 | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: Cross-patient access is denied (403/404 per policy); only the in-context patient is retrievable

Title: Inbound HL7 ADT from unauthorized sending facility is rejected
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine configured to accept only allow-listed sending applications/facilities
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 from a non-allow-listed MSH-4 sending facility | 2. Observe ACK
Expected_Result: Interface returns an application reject ACK (AR) and does not process the patient admit

Title: HL7 ORM order from a sender lacking order-entry authorization is blocked
Preconditions: HL7 interface restricts order messages to authorized clinical systems
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORM^O01 order from a sending application not authorized for orders | 2. Observe ACK and downstream order state
Expected_Result: Order message is rejected with a negative ACK; no order is created in the receiving system

Title: Manage Users form is fully keyboard navigable
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; on Add/Edit User form
Test_Steps: 1. Place focus at page start | 2. Tab through all fields and controls | 3. Operate role checkboxes and Save using keyboard only
Expected_Result: All interactive elements are reachable and operable via keyboard in a logical order with a visible focus indicator

Title: Role assignment controls expose accessible names to screen readers
Preconditions: Screen reader active; on Add User role selection
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate the role checkboxes with a screen reader | 2. Listen to announced labels and states
Expected_Result: Each role control announces a meaningful accessible name and its checked/unchecked state

Title: Access-denied error message is announced and not color-only
Preconditions: A non-admin user triggering an insufficient-privilege error
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger an access-denied error | 2. Verify the message is conveyed via text and ARIA, not color alone | 3. Check screen reader announcement
Expected_Result: The error is presented as text with sufficient contrast and announced to assistive technology, not relying on color only

Title: Removing a privilege from a role revokes access for its users
Preconditions: Role Nurse has Edit Patients; a nurse user can currently edit demographics
Test_Steps: 1. As admin remove Edit Patients from the Nurse role | 2. Save | 3. As the nurse re-attempt to edit demographics
Expected_Result: After removal, the nurse can no longer edit demographics; the action becomes denied

Title: Inherited privilege removal cascades to child roles
Preconditions: Parent role Nurse grants View Clinical; child role Senior Nurse inherits it
Test_Steps: 1. Remove View Clinical from Nurse | 2. Save | 3. Inspect effective privileges of Senior Nurse and its user
Expected_Result: Senior Nurse loses the inherited privilege unless directly assigned; its users lose corresponding access

Title: Explore privilege gaps by toggling individual privileges on a sandbox role
Preconditions: Admin access; a sandbox role and test user available
Test_Steps: 1. Assign sandbox role one privilege at a time | 2. Probe which UI features and endpoints unlock | 3. Note any feature accessible without an obvious matching privilege
Expected_Result: Each unlocked feature maps to an intended privilege; any feature reachable without a guarding privilege is flagged as a defect

Title: Explore behavior when a user holds conflicting allow/deny role intents
Preconditions: Admin access; ability to assign multiple roles to one user
Test_Steps: 1. Assign a user two roles whose privilege sets overlap and diverge | 2. Attempt boundary actions | 3. Observe how the system resolves the union
Expected_Result: System resolves privileges as a permissive union consistently; document the effective behavior and any surprising grants

Title: Pharmacist can dispense but cannot register new patients
Preconditions: Logged in as a user with only the Pharmacist role
Test_Steps: 1. Open dispensing/medication workflow and confirm access | 2. Attempt to open Register a Patient
Expected_Result: Pharmacist can perform dispensing actions but the Register a Patient app is unavailable/denied

Title: Report viewer role can run reports but cannot edit patient data
Preconditions: Logged in as a user with only the Report Viewer role
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Reports app and run a report | 2. Attempt to open a patient and edit data
Expected_Result: Reports run successfully; any attempt to edit patient data is denied

Title: Username boundary at minimum allowed length
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; username policy defines a minimum length
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a username one character below the minimum and try to save | 2. Then enter a username at exactly the minimum and save
Expected_Result: Below-minimum username is rejected; the exact-minimum username is accepted and the user is created

Title: Reject username containing invalid characters
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; on Add User screen
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a username with disallowed special characters | 2. Set a valid password | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the invalid username with a format validation error and creates no user

Title: Stored XSS attempt in role description is neutralized
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; on Add Role
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a script payload in the role description | 2. Save | 3. View the role in a list and detail page
Expected_Result: The payload is escaped/encoded on output; no script executes when the role is viewed

Title: SQL injection in user search is not executed
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; on Manage Users search
Test_Steps: 1. Enter an SQL injection string into the user search field | 2. Submit | 3. Observe results and logs
Expected_Result: Input is treated as literal text; no unauthorized data is returned and no SQL error/exposure occurs

Title: Orphaned role-privilege mapping is not left after privilege deletion
Preconditions: Admin access with DB read; a privilege assigned to a role exists
Test_Steps: 1. Delete a privilege that is assigned to a role (if permitted) | 2. Query the role_privilege mapping table | 3. Verify referential integrity
Expected_Result: No dangling role-privilege rows remain; deletion is either blocked or cascades cleanly preserving integrity

Title: User password is stored hashed and salted, never plaintext
Preconditions: Admin access with DB read; a user with a known password exists
Test_Steps: 1. Create a user with a known password | 2. Query the user/credential table | 3. Inspect the stored password value
Expected_Result: The stored credential is a salted hash, not the plaintext password; no reversible password is persisted

Title: Disabling the last administrator account is prevented
Preconditions: Only one active System Administrator account remains
Test_Steps: 1. As that admin, attempt to disable/retire the sole remaining admin account | 2. Observe outcome
Expected_Result: System prevents removing the last administrator, avoiding a lockout with no privileged account remaining

Title: Anonymous (unauthenticated) user cannot reach any patient data
Preconditions: No user session; application login page reachable
Test_Steps: 1. Without logging in, navigate directly to a patient dashboard URL | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: User is redirected to login; no patient data is exposed to the anonymous request

Title: Linking a user to a provider record enables provider-scoped actions
Preconditions: Admin access; a user exists; a provider record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Providers | 2. Link the provider to the user account | 3. Log in as the user and attempt a provider-required action (e.g. be selected as ordering provider)
Expected_Result: The user becomes selectable as a provider and can perform provider-scoped clinical actions

Title: Retiring a provider removes them from active provider selection lists
Preconditions: Admin access; an active provider exists and appears in selection lists
Test_Steps: 1. Retire the provider in Manage Providers | 2. Open an order/encounter provider dropdown | 3. Search for the retired provider
Expected_Result: The retired provider no longer appears in active selection lists; existing references remain intact

Title: Concurrent logins for same user honored per policy
Preconditions: Concurrent-session policy defined; user jnurse exists
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as jnurse in two browsers | 2. Perform an action in each | 3. Observe whether both sessions are permitted or one is invalidated per policy
Expected_Result: Behavior matches the configured concurrent-session policy consistently (both allowed, or earliest invalidated)

Title: Editing a role description does not alter its privilege set
Preconditions: Admin access; an existing role with known privileges
Test_Steps: 1. Open the role | 2. Change only the description | 3. Save | 4. Reopen and verify privileges unchanged
Expected_Result: Description updates while the privilege set remains exactly as before

Title: Read-only auditor role can view audit logs but cannot modify users
Preconditions: User with an Auditor role granting audit view but not user management
Test_Steps: 1. Open the audit log view and confirm read access | 2. Attempt to open Add/Edit User
Expected_Result: Auditor can read audit data; user-management actions are denied

Title: Newly created user is forced to change password on first login
Preconditions: Admin access; force-change-on-first-login policy enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Create a user with a temporary password | 2. Log in as that user for the first time | 3. Observe the prompt
Expected_Result: User is required to set a new password before accessing the application

Title: User retains correct access after role rename
Preconditions: Admin access; role Clerk assigned to a user
Test_Steps: 1. Rename the role Clerk to Front Desk | 2. Save | 3. Log in as the assigned user and verify privileges
Expected_Result: The user keeps the same effective privileges after the rename; access is unaffected by the name change

Title: Clerk can register a patient but cannot delete a patient
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk with Add Patients but not Delete Patients
Test_Steps: 1. Register a new patient successfully | 2. Open the patient and attempt to delete/void the patient record
Expected_Result: Registration succeeds; the delete/void patient action is unavailable or denied

Title: Privilege-protected REST endpoint enforces same rule as UI
Preconditions: Nurse credentials lacking Delete Encounters; REST module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm in UI the void-encounter control is hidden for the nurse | 2. Call the equivalent REST DELETE/void as the nurse | 3. Compare outcomes
Expected_Result: REST enforces the same authorization as the UI and returns 403; no encounter is voided

Title: Password reset by admin is logged without exposing the new password
Preconditions: Admin access; audit logging enabled; user jnurse exists
Test_Steps: 1. Reset jnurse password as admin | 2. Inspect the audit log entry | 3. Confirm content
Expected_Result: Audit records the reset event with actor, target, and timestamp; the new password value is not stored in the log

Title: API parameter tampering cannot assign a privilege the actor lacks
Preconditions: Data Manager credentials that can edit roles but should not grant privileges they do not themselves hold
Test_Steps: 1. As Data Manager, send a role-update API call adding a high-risk privilege they lack | 2. Observe response and resulting role
Expected_Result: Server rejects assigning a privilege the actor does not hold (no privilege escalation); role is unchanged

Title: Disabled app/privilege hides corresponding home dashboard tile
Preconditions: Admin access; a user whose only role grants one app
Test_Steps: 1. Remove that app's guarding privilege from the role | 2. Save | 3. Log in as the user and view the dashboard
Test_Data: Role with single app privilege removed
Expected_Result: The corresponding app tile no longer appears on the user's dashboard
